DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. ----- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND THE BASIS FOR THE RULING STATED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING --- DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THE QUESTION REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S IMMIGRATION STATUS IS RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES. RODRIGUEZ V. KLINE (1986) 186 CAL.APP.3D 1145. THE 2002 AMENDMENTS WERE ENACTED TO ADDRESS HOFFMAN PLASTIC COMPOUNDS, INC. WHICH DEALT WITH AN ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT'S ABILITY TO OBTAIN HIS/HER BACKWAGES WITHOUT FEAR OF SEEKING REDRESS BECAUSE OF THEIR IMMIGRATION STATUS. MOREOVER, THE DISCOVERY STANDARD IS BROADER THAN THE ADMISSIBILITY STANDARD UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 352 WHICH CAN BE RAISED AT TRIAL. ANY QUESTIONING OF PLAINTIFF ON HIS IMMIGRATION STATUS MUST BE NARROWLY TAILORED TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES AND NOTHING FURTHER. FURTHERMORE, THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT THE SPECIFIC QUESTION PRESENTED IN THIS MOTION AND ANY RELATED QUESTION SHALL BE PRESENTED TO THE PLAINTIFF IN A NON-HARASSING, NONTHREATENING MANNER, AND SHALL BE DONE WITH ONLY COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES AND THE COURT REPORTER PRESENT. FURTHER, THE QUESTIONING SHALL BE VERY LIMITED IN SCOPE AND ONLY NECESSARY AND RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES. FINALLY, ONCE THE QUESTIONS ARE ASKED AND ANSWERED, THAT PORTION OF THE DEPOSITION SHALL BE SEALED AND NOT VIEWED BY ANYONE EXCEPT THE COURT, THE PLAINTIFF, AND COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT OR THE TRIAL COURT THAT THIS MATTER MAY BE ASSIGNED TO IN THE FUTURE. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AN ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE REFLECTING THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ELISEO TURCIOS MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - HEARD ON DISCOVERY CALENDAR. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Answers To Deposition Questions And Appendix Of Non-California Authorities
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS.

APPEARANCES: John M. Anton, Esq. of Boxer Gerson 510/835-8870 & Conchita Lozano-Batista, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld 510/337-1001 for pltfs & opposing parties; Mark J. Hancock, Esq. of Sedgwick, Detert, Moran & Arnold LLP 781-7900 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: The Court adopts its tentative ruling and the basis for the ruling stated in the tentative ruling --- DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THE QUESTION REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S IMMIGRATION STATUS IS RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES. RODRIGUEZ V. KLINE (1986) 186 CAL.APP.3D 1145. THE 2002 AMENDMENTS WERE ENACTED TO ADDRESS HOFFMAN PLASTIC COMPOUNDS, INC. WHICH DEALT WITH AN ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT'S ABILITY TO OBTAIN HIS/HER BACKWAGES WITHOUT FEAR OF SEEKING REDRESS BECAUSE OF THEIR IMMIGRATION STATUS. MOREOVER, THE DISCOVERY STANDARD IS BROADER THAN THE ADMISSIBILITY STANDARD UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 352 WHICH CAN BE RAISED AT TRIAL. ANY QUESTIONING OF PLAINTIFF ON HIS IMMIGRATION STATUS MUST BE NARROWLY TAILORED TO THE ISSUE OF FUTURE WAGES AND NOTHING FURTHER.
Furthermore, the Court further orders that the specific question presented in this motion and any related question shall be presented to the plaintiff in a non-harassing, nonthreatening manner, and shall be done with only counsel for the parties and the court reporter present. Further, the questioning shall be very limited in scope and only necessary and relevant to the issue of future wages.
Finally, once the questions are asked and answered, that portion of the deposition shall be sealed and not viewed by anyone except the Court, the plaintiff, and counsel for the parties until further order of this Court or the trial Court that this matter may be assigned to in the future.
Counsel for the defendant shall prepare an order for the Court's signature reflecting the Court's ruling.
Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
